S
Total Breakthrough
Training
Seven SI Coaching Stages
Mark Peysha and Magali Peysha
with commentary by Cloe Madanes
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 1 of 18 --

First Session
During your first session you learned about your client’s life.
What and who matters to them.
In your second coaching session you will choose a coaching
path and priority to follow for several sessions.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 18 --

7 SI Coaching Stages
1. 	Understanding and information gathering about behavior.
2. 	Reframe, what do you call this, relate it to a need, life stage, a problem which
can be solved. (purpose/result)
3. 	Action- (Pattern interrupt) SI Strategies.
4. 	Expanding unit- who is client’s natural support. Who would bring greater
strength or new habits in.
5. 	Adding – Who can I meet, and or deepen relationships with. What can I do?
Where do I find strength?
6. 	Possibilities- Growth, emotions, dreams.
7. 	3 A’s- Accept, Anchor, Anticipate challenges
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 18 --

4 SI Coaching Paths
1. 	Behavior/Pattern
2. 	Outcome/Goal
3. 	State/emotion
4. 	Relationship
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 18 --

Coaching path-Behaviors/
Patterns using 7 SI Stages
Timing
Triggers
Key players
Secondary Gain
Consequences
1st Stage- What you need to understand
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 18 --

2nd Stage Reframe
See the purpose that problem behavior served.
Retool behavior for helpful results. Finding purpose.
State behavior or term in words in which it can be solved.
You can do this by making it a need, value, or past key decision which
you can revisit and change.
How you do this depends on your style as a coach. One coach may talk
from the perspective of their experience. Another may say this is how it
is. A third could ask for the opinions of a fairy godparent who once
loved the client.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 18 --

3rd Stage SI Strategy
An Action which person takes that changes their perception of
their behavior, their state, timing, and triggers.
Pretend- making it deliberate
Time of day (ordeal)- you have to do the behavior
Consequence- Change who benefits from behavior
Meaning/needs- Change the purpose or reasoning or emotion
for the action/behavior.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 18 --

4th Stage Expand the Unit
Gaining different perspectives about behavior. How does your husband feel
about … questions.
Who or what would you change for?
Who or what are they reacting to?
Finding key supportive relationships that have helped in the past. Also,
behaviors which have helped in the past.
Times of life with out behavior who was part of your life.
This can be in conversations with your client, through phone calls, family
sessions.
Actions- Give and help others in the client’s life.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 18 --

5th Stage Adding
A solution is sometimes completely different from a problem.
A terrible behavior can be sign of a inbalance. So you look for a
way to add in balance.
How this can work. Anxiety problem. Focus is on work,
performance. Coach decides its critical to focus on health and
getting stronger.
When adding in you always want it to expand the persons world
view, relationships, emotional, and physical strength.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 18 --

6th Stage Possibilities- Growth,
emotions, dreams.
Archetype strategy- Integrating different parts of a person.
Seeking wisdom, warrior, magic, lover to help ground and
strengthen.
Future thinking- How would a perfect day look. How would a
great relationship feel, be.
Brainstorm- All the opportunities which could come about
Metaphorical play- what can a problem metamorphose into
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 18 --

7th SI Stage- 3 A’s- Accept,
Anchor, Anticipate challenges
When client sees new perspective, self, experience. Accept is to
love, take in, experience as part of oneself, know that they are
different from a behavior or pattern.
Anchoring new self, behavior, relationships, priorities, purpose.
Anticipate Challenges- Who could this threaten. When will I
need my old behavior or pattern and how will I still give and
love myself, or cope with my life.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 18 --

Coaching path- outcome/goals
using 7 SI Stages
1st Stage- What you need to understand
How does this work in world- reverse engineering
If I get this what would I do in reverse to be there.
What are my behaviors, beliefs, relationships, rules, and fears that are in
my way.
Truth in client’s self criticism? Access severity of the problem. How far
or close are they to doing what it takes to reach goal.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 18 --

2nd Stage Reframe
Coach brings client a new focus and context for their goal and outcome. Purpose is
to attain goal but in doable phases and steps.
You look at: Life cycle, Career cycle, and Life stage
Relationship cycle- What comes between single – married
What comes between fighting with teen- Great times with teen
6 human needs – is there another way to meet the need behind the present goal right
now
Understanding the clients strengths versus interests
I have to publish a book- Reframe would be- I need to write two pages a day.
You take an imperative (out of control goal) and turn into a actionable step with
phases to meet goal or outcome.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 18 --

3rd Action- (Pattern interrupt) SI
Strategies.
Swallow the Frog
Reverse engineering- 10 years and back to day by day
Memory modification- confusing bad thoughts or memories
Incremental rewards- Stop holding out on yourself and start
giving
Interviewing others who’ve attained similar goals or outcomes
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 18 --

4th Stage- Expanding the Unit
Dedication of goal/outcome to another person- Who is this for
and how will it help them.
Mentor and or growth community
Accountability partner
Greater purpose
Understand road blocks- cultural, relationship, family, personal
fears of success, cultural stereotypes.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 18 --

5th Stage - Adding
If an action makes a person feel guilty it isn’t a adding activity.
Down to earth simple actions which give strength, happiness,
balance and inspiration.
Making new friends, learning a new language, incorporating
relaxing, entertaining activities into day or week.
Giving oneself a vacation day.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 18 --

6th Stage- Possibilities- Growth,
emotions, dreams.
Visualization of outcome
Moving towards instead of away from do to fear
Prioritizing within the desired goal
Stages- break down of goals into parts with reward rituals
This can be done daily and at end of a session.
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 18 --

7th Stage- 3 A’s- Accept, Anchor,
Anticipate challenges
What is great and more important than this goal or outcome.
Gratitude right now. How do I enjoy the journey today?
Daily Strength Process
Anchor through strengths- I can do this because I am…
Even if or when this doesn’t happen like I expect, or when
something gets in the way, I will think… I will be resilient by
doing…
www.getwsodownload.com	www.getwsodownload.com
